title: ParseContextIntentResolved payload
description: Emitted by the parse_context handler on every call that ran the directive-intent detector (chain parse-context-lean-orienting T5). T10's fire-rate, detection-path mix, and latency measurements consume this event. message_hash is a SHA-256 truncation so downstream analytics can dedupe identical re-resolutions without retaining the raw user prompt.

intent_shape.description: The resolved intent shape from DetectIntent. Closed set per PARSE_CONTEXT.md §13.2 — emitting an out-of-set value is rejected at the schema layer.

detection_path.description: Which detection mechanism produced the shape. 'pattern' = the pure-regex fast path; 'rubric' = Qwen rubric fallback (T4 §13.5 opt-in, not shipped today); 'hybrid' = both fired and combined; 'default' = no detector matched and IntentNone is the fallback.

latency_ms.description: Wall-clock cost of the intent-detection pass in milliseconds. Sub-millisecond on the pattern path; meaningful when the rubric path lands. T10's latency regression dashboard reads this directly.

fallback_to_none.description: True when the detector ran but no shape matched (IntentNone returned as default). Distinguishes 'detector evaluated and abstained' from 'detector evaluated and decided X' for T10's fire-rate accounting.

message_hash.description: SHA-256 hex of the message text, truncated to 16 chars. Stable identifier for dedup analytics; deliberately not the raw prompt to avoid retaining PII / sensitive content beyond the live envelope.
